description: Trigger discrimination for DOC lifecycle/retention/retirement (this skill) vs library-SKILL retirement (skill-deprecation-planner — the pinned seam, already referenced from its side), product-feature/API sunset to users (sunset-deprecation-communicator), and corpus organization by mode (diataxis-doc-organizer).

cases.prompt: Govern our workflow docs' lifecycle: a numbered index with retention category, reason-to-keep, superseded-by, and cleanup rule, plus a safe retirement process.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — retention categories, reason-to-keep, and staged doc retirement govern DOC lifecycle. Not a skill, not a product feature.
cases.reason: Governing the retention and retirement of workflow/process docs is this skill's core scope.

cases.prompt: Deprecate the 'foo-analyzer' skill: reverse-link sweep across neighbor trigger-evals, mark→redirect→remove, and move its catalog/README rows to a retired record.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary, reverse — the pinned seam) — the object is a library SKILL with a neighbor-trigger-eval reverse-link graph and catalog rows, not a workflow doc.
cases.reason: skill-deprecation-planner retires a library skill (it already pins docs-retention-index from its side). This skill governs docs; the reverse-link graph and registration mechanics differ.

cases.prompt: Plan how we deprecate and sunset a customer-facing feature: rationale, impact, migration path, timeline, and the communication plan.
cases.reason: sunset-deprecation-communicator sunsets a product feature/API for external users (comms + migration). This skill governs internal doc retention, not user communication.

cases.prompt: Sort our documentation into tutorials, how-to, reference, and explanation.
cases.reason: diataxis-doc-organizer classifies docs by type. This skill governs their lifecycle (keep/supersede/retire), a different axis.
